content.content:
[closed] Issue #7: CI + release binary: GitHub Actions, cross-compile, release artifacts
  Author: @gabadi
  Status: CLOSED
  URL: https://github.com/gabadi/drywall/issues/7

  Description:
    ## Parent
    
    #1 — PRD: drywall — polyglot AST subtree DRY analyzer
    
    ## What to build
    
    Wire up GitHub Actions CI and produce cross-compiled release binaries. Every push runs build + tests + the dogfood gate. Tagged releases produce downloadable binaries for Linux, macOS, and Windows with no runtime dependencies.
    
    ```gherkin
    Given a push to any branch
    When CI runs
    Then the project builds without errors
    And all tests pass
    And `drywall ./src` exits 0 (dogfood gate)
    
    Given a Linux CI environment with no Rust, Node.js, or Python installed
    When the release binary is downloaded and run on a repository
    Then it produces output without any runtime dependency errors
    
    Given a git tag is pushed
    When CI runs
    Then downloadable binaries are attached to a GitHub Release for:
      - x86_64-unknown-linux-musl
      - x86_64-apple-darwin
      - aarch64-apple-darwin
      - x86_64-pc-windows-msvc
    ```
    
    ### CI pipeline steps (on every push)
    
    1. `cargo build --release`
    2. `cargo test`
    3. `./target/release/drywall ./src` — dogfood gate, must exit 0
    
    ### Release pipeline (on tag push)
    
    Cross-compile to all four targets and attach binaries to a GitHub Release. Use `cross` or `cargo-zigbuild` for Linux musl static builds.
    
    ## Acceptance criteria
    
    - [ ] Push to any branch triggers build + test + dogfood in CI
    - [ ] CI fails if dogfood gate (`drywall ./src`) exits non-zero
    - [ ] Tagged release produces binaries for Linux (musl static), macOS x86_64, macOS arm64, Windows x86_64
    - [ ] Linux binary has no dynamic libc dependency (`ldd` shows statically linked)
    - [ ] Binaries downloadable from the GitHub Release page
    
    ## Blocked by
    
    #5 and #6

file.content:
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:

env:
  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always

permissions:
  pull-requests: write

jobs:
  ci:
    name: CI
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683 # v4.2.2
      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@3c5f7ea28cd621ae0bf5283f0e981fb97b8a7af9 # master
        with:
          toolchain: "1.96.0"
          components: clippy, rustfmt, llvm-tools-preview
      - uses: taiki-e/install-action@15449e3094499af05d8d964a1c884208e4b8b595 # v2
        with:
          tool: cargo-nextest@0.9.137,cargo-llvm-cov@0.8.7,cargo-crap@0.2.2
      - uses: actions/cache@5a3ec84eff668545956fd18022155c47e93e2684 # v4.2.3
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cargo/registry
            ~/.cargo/git
            target
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-${{ hashFiles('**/Cargo.lock') }}
          restore-keys: ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-
      - name: Format check
        run: cargo fmt --check
      - name: Clippy
        run: cargo clippy -- -D warnings
      - name: Unit tests
        run: cargo nextest run --profile unit
      - name: Install lcov
        run: sudo apt-get install -y lcov
      - name: Coverage
        run: cargo llvm-cov nextest --profile unit --lcov --output-path lcov.info --fail-under-lines 90
      - name: Coverage report
        uses: zgosalvez/github-actions-report-lcov@eda775f552bfb2bffbdedea26f19dfb8c24a1648 # v4
        if: always()
        with:
          coverage-files: lcov.info
          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
      - name: CRAP
        run: cargo crap --lcov lcov.info --exclude 'acceptance/**' --threshold 6 --fail-above
      - name: Build release binary
        run: cargo build --release
      - name: DRY
        run: ./target/release/drywall ./src
      - name: Property tests
        run: cargo nextest run --profile property
      - name: Acceptance tests
        run: cargo nextest run --profile acceptance

Here's the full picture:

**drywall is already better designed for CI than dry4go/dry4clj.** Key finding: in unclebob's repos those tools aren't CI gates at all — agents run them manually. And dry4go exits 0 whether or not duplicates are found, so you'd have to pipe-parse output to use it as a gate. drywall already exits 1 on duplicates, 0 on clean — a proper hard gate.

**The only gap is distribution.** Right now adding drywall to an external CI pipeline requires a Rust toolchain, which is a non-starter. We need cross-compiled binaries.

**What to build:**
- A tag-triggered GitHub Actions release workflow that cross-compiles to:
  - `x86_64-unknown-linux-musl` (static, no libc dependency)
  - `aarch64-apple-darwin` (Apple Silicon)
  - `x86_64-apple-darwin` (Intel Mac)
- Attaches binaries to a GitHub Release
- Optionally: a one-liner install script (`curl | sh`) for CI pipelines

**Usage in a target project's CI would then be:**
```yaml
- run: curl -sL https://github.com/gabadi/drywall/releases/latest/download/drywall-linux-x86_64 -o drywall && chmod +x drywall
- run: ./drywall ./src
```

name: Release

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - 'v*'

env:
  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always

permissions:
  contents: write

jobs:
  build:
    name: Build ${{ matrix.target }}
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      matrix:
        include:
          - target: x86_64-unknown-linux-musl
            os: ubuntu-latest
            artifact: drywall-linux-x86_64
          - target: aarch64-apple-darwin
            os: macos-latest
            artifact: drywall-macos-aarch64
          - target: x86_64-apple-darwin
            os: macos-13
            artifact: drywall-macos-x86_64

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683 # v4.2.2

      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@3c5f7ea28cd621ae0bf5283f0e981fb97b8a7af9 # master
        with:
          toolchain: "1.96.0"
          targets: ${{ matrix.target }}

      - name: Install musl tools
        if: matrix.target == 'x86_64-unknown-linux-musl'
        run: sudo apt-get install -y musl-tools

      - uses: actions/cache@5a3ec84eff668545956fd18022155c47e93e2684 # v4.2.3
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cargo/registry
            ~/.cargo/git
            target
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-${{ matrix.target }}-cargo-${{ hashFiles('**/Cargo.lock') }}
          restore-keys: ${{ runner.os }}-${{ matrix.target }}-cargo-

      - name: Build
        run: cargo build --release --target ${{ matrix.target }}

      - name: Rename binary
        run: cp target/${{ matrix.target }}/release/drywall ${{ matrix.artifact }}

      - name: Upload artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4
        with:
          name: ${{ matrix.artifact }}
          path: ${{ matrix.artifact }}

  release:
    name: Create Release
    needs: build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Download artifacts
        uses: actions/download-artifact@d3f86a106a0bac45b974a628896c90dbdf5c8093 # v4
        with:
          merge-multiple: true

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@da05d552573ad5aba039eaac05058a918a7bf631 # v2
        with:
          files: |
            drywall-linux-x86_64
            drywall-macos-aarch64
            drywall-macos-x86_64
          generate_release_notes: true

1	# AGENTS.md
2	
3	## Tool name mappings
4	
5	Prompts use generic names; use the installed binaries:
6	
7	- `ir-dry-checker` → `gherkin-ir-dry-checker`
8	
9	`gherkin-ir-dry-checker` takes JSON IR from `gherkin-parser`, not the `.feature`:
10	
11	```
12	gherkin-parser <feature> <ir.json>
13	gherkin-ir-dry-checker [--include-exact] <ir.json> <report>
14	```
15	
16	`findings: []` = already normalized. Also installed: `gherkin-parser`, `gherkin-mutator`.
17	
18	## Command index
19	
20	| Purpose | Command |
21	|---------|---------|
22	| Install tools (once per machine) | `mise install` |
23	| Format | `cargo fmt` |
24	| Lint | `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` |
25	| Unit tests | `cargo nextest run --profile unit` |
26	| Property tests | `cargo nextest run --profile property` |
27	| Acceptance tests | `cargo nextest run --profile acceptance` |
28	| Language mutation | `cargo mutants --test-tool nextest -j 8 --no-shuffle -- --profile mutation` |
29	| Gherkin mutation | `gherkin-mutator --level soft --feature <feature>` |
30	| Coverage (≥90% lines) | `mise exec -- cargo llvm-cov nextest --profile unit --lcov --output-path lcov.info --fail-under-lines 90` |
31	| CRAP (threshold ≤6) | `cargo crap --lcov lcov.info --exclude 'acceptance/**' --exclude 'src/main.rs' --threshold 6 --fail-above` |
32	| Build release binary | `cargo build --release` |
33	| DRY self-check | `./target/release/drywall ./src` |
34	
35	## Mutation runs
36	
37	**Always launch mutation commands with `run_in_background: true`.** Do not block waiting for mutation output — it takes several minutes. Check results when the background notification arrives.
38	
39	## Tooling notes
40	
41	- **gherkin-ir-dry-checker output**: use `rtk json <report>` to read the JSON report compactly rather than dumping full JSON into context.
42	- **gherkin-mutator**: `--feature-file` and `--feature-path` are wrong flag names and silently run 0 mutations; use `--feature`.
43	- **Build release binary before acceptance**: `cargo build --release` must run before `--profile acceptance` — stale binary causes false failures.
44	- **acceptance-entrypoint-generator**: requires `--steps <steps-file>` (basename only) for non-scaffold features; omitting it defaults to `scaffold_cli_steps.rs` and silently generates the wrong entrypoint.
45	- **cargo-mutants `--exclude`**: patterns match absolute paths, not CWD-relative. Use `**/filename.rs` form, not `src/filename.rs`.
46	- **acceptance/runtime/mod.rs `World`**: add `#[allow(dead_code)]` on the struct (not per-field) — runtime is `include!`-ed across multiple test binaries with differing step coverage.
47	- **Acceptance fixture paths**: must be outside gitignored paths (e.g. not under `tmp/`) — gitignore-awareness is default-on and silently skips ignored dirs, producing false-empty scans.
48	- **Ad-hoc QA scratch fixtures**: write to `/tmp` or a non-gitignored path; any fixture under project `tmp/` is silently skipped by drywall — produces false exit 0 mimicking a missing feature.
49	- **Parallel language test bodies (js_X / ts_X)**: differentiate source body structure, not just identifiers — identical short bodies trigger dogfood self-detection even when function names differ.
50	- **`Lang::Tsx` grammar**: must use `LANGUAGE_TSX`, not `LANGUAGE_TYPESCRIPT`; `typescript_grammar_rejects_jsx_markup` is the guard — removing or weakening it silently breaks JSX in .tsx.
51	- **acceptance-entrypoint-generator absolute paths**: when called with an output dir outside the project root (e.g. `/tmp/`), generated `include!` paths become absolute. Fix: write output directly into `tests/` or apply `sed` to relativize paths before committing.
52	
53	## drywall output format
54	
55	drywall output mirrors dry4go: per-function node counts, one function per line. Sort order for ties is unspecified upstream; drywall pins lexicographic tie-break to guarantee deterministic output for mutation testing.

# AGENTS.md

## Tool name mappings

Prompts use generic names; use the installed binaries:

- `ir-dry-checker` → `gherkin-ir-dry-checker`

`gherkin-ir-dry-checker` takes JSON IR from `gherkin-parser`, not the `.feature`:

```
gherkin-parser <feature> <ir.json>
gherkin-ir-dry-checker [--include-exact] <ir.json> <report>
```

`findings: []` = already normalized. Also installed: `gherkin-parser`, `gherkin-mutator`.

## Command index

| Purpose | Command |
|---------|---------|
| Install tools (once per machine) | `mise install` |
| Format | `cargo fmt` |
| Lint | `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` |
| Unit tests | `cargo nextest run --profile unit` |
| Property tests | `cargo nextest run --profile property` |
| Acceptance tests | `cargo nextest run --profile acceptance` |
| Language mutation | `cargo mutants --test-tool nextest -j 8 --no-shuffle -- --profile mutation` |
| Gherkin mutation | `gherkin-mutator --level soft --feature <feature>` |
| Coverage (≥90% lines) | `mise exec -- cargo llvm-cov nextest --profile unit --lcov --output-path lcov.info --fail-under-lines 90` |
| CRAP (threshold ≤6) | `cargo crap --lcov lcov.info --exclude 'acceptance/**' --exclude 'src/main.rs' --threshold 6 --fail-above` |
| Build release binary | `cargo build --release` |
| DRY self-check | `./target/release/drywall ./src` |

## Mutation runs

**Always launch mutation commands with `run_in_background: true`.** Do not block waiting for mutation output — it takes several minutes. Check results when the background notification arrives.

## Tooling notes

- **gherkin-ir-dry-checker output**: use `rtk json <report>` to read the JSON report compactly rather than dumping full JSON into context.
- **gherkin-mutator**: `--feature-file` and `--feature-path` are wrong flag names and silently run 0 mutations; use `--feature`.
- **Build release binary before acceptance**: `cargo build --release` must run before `--profile acceptance` — stale binary causes false failures.
- **acceptance-entrypoint-generator**: requires `--steps <steps-file>` (basename only) for non-scaffold features; omitting it defaults to `scaffold_cli_steps.rs` and silently generates the wrong entrypoint.
- **cargo-mutants `--exclude`**: patterns match absolute paths, not CWD-relative. Use `**/filename.rs` form, not `src/filename.rs`.

- **Acceptance fixture paths**: must be outside gitignored paths (e.g. not under `tmp/`) — gitignore-awareness is default-on and silently skips ignored dirs, producing false-empty scans.

- **Parallel language test bodies (js_X / ts_X)**: differentiate source body structure, not just identifiers — identical short bodies trigger dogfood self-detection even when function names differ.

## drywall output format

name: Release

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - 'v*'

env:
  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always

permissions:
  contents: write

jobs:
  build:
    name: Build ${{ matrix.target }}
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      matrix:
        include:
          - target: x86_64-unknown-linux-musl
            os: ubuntu-latest
            artifact: drywall-linux-x86_64
          - target: aarch64-apple-darwin
            os: macos-latest
            artifact: drywall-macos-aarch64
          - target: x86_64-apple-darwin
            os: macos-13
            artifact: drywall-macos-x86_64

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683 # v4.2.2

      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@3c5f7ea28cd621ae0bf5283f0e981fb97b8a7af9 # master
        with:
          toolchain: "1.96.0"
          targets: ${{ matrix.target }}

      - name: Install musl tools
        if: matrix.target == 'x86_64-unknown-linux-musl'
        run: sudo apt-get install -y musl-tools

      - uses: actions/cache@5a3ec84eff668545956fd18022155c47e93e2684 # v4.2.3
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cargo/registry
            ~/.cargo/git
            target
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-${{ matrix.target }}-cargo-${{ hashFiles('**/Cargo.lock') }}
          restore-keys: ${{ runner.os }}-${{ matrix.target }}-cargo-

      - name: Build
        run: cargo build --release --target ${{ matrix.target }}

      - name: Rename binary
        run: cp target/${{ matrix.target }}/release/drywall ${{ matrix.artifact }}

      - name: Upload artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: ${{ matrix.artifact }}
          path: ${{ matrix.artifact }}

  release:
    name: Create Release
    needs: build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Download artifacts
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          merge-multiple: true

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          files: |
            drywall-linux-x86_64
            drywall-macos-aarch64
            drywall-macos-x86_64
          generate_release_notes: true

input.content:
# drywall

Polyglot AST subtree DRY analyzer. Detects duplicate code across files using Jaccard similarity over normalized AST fingerprints.

## Install

Download the binary for your platform from the [latest release](https://github.com/gabadi/drywall/releases/latest):

| Platform | Binary |
|----------|--------|
| Linux x86_64 | `drywall-linux-x86_64` |
| macOS Apple Silicon | `drywall-macos-aarch64` |
| macOS Intel | `drywall-macos-x86_64` |

```bash
# Linux
curl -sL https://github.com/gabadi/drywall/releases/latest/download/drywall-linux-x86_64 -o drywall
chmod +x ./drywall

# macOS Apple Silicon
curl -sL https://github.com/gabadi/drywall/releases/latest/download/drywall-macos-aarch64 -o drywall
chmod +x ./drywall

# macOS Intel
curl -sL https://github.com/gabadi/drywall/releases/latest/download/drywall-macos-x86_64 -o drywall
chmod +x ./drywall
```

The Linux binary is statically linked (musl) — no runtime dependencies.

## Usage

```bash
drywall ./src
```

Exit codes: `0` = no duplicates, `1` = duplicates found, `2` = bad arguments.

## Use as a CI gate

Add to any project's CI pipeline:

```yaml
- name: Install drywall
  run: |
    curl -sL https://github.com/gabadi/drywall/releases/latest/download/drywall-linux-x86_64 -o drywall
    chmod +x ./drywall

- name: DRY check
  run: ./drywall ./src
```

The step fails (exit 1) if duplicates are found.

## Build from source

Requires Rust 1.96+:

```bash
cargo build --release
./target/release/drywall ./src
```

content.text:
drywall already has `--threshold` (default 0.82, same as dry4go). So in CI:

```yaml
- run: ./drywall --threshold 0.82 ./src
```

Lower threshold = stricter (flags more pairs). `0.1` would flag nearly anything with any overlap — probably too aggressive. The dry4go default of `0.82` is the established convention for "meaningfully duplicated."

# drywall

Polyglot AST subtree DRY analyzer. Detects duplicate code across files using Jaccard similarity over normalized AST fingerprints.

## Install

Download the binary for your platform from the [latest release](https://github.com/gabadi/drywall/releases/latest):

| Platform | Binary |
|----------|--------|
| Linux x86_64 | `drywall-linux-x86_64` |
| macOS Apple Silicon | `drywall-macos-aarch64` |
| macOS Intel | `drywall-macos-x86_64` |

```bash
# Linux
curl -sL https://github.com/gabadi/drywall/releases/latest/download/drywall-linux-x86_64 -o drywall
chmod +x ./drywall

# macOS Apple Silicon
curl -sL https://github.com/gabadi/drywall/releases/latest/download/drywall-macos-aarch64 -o drywall
chmod +x ./drywall

# macOS Intel
curl -sL https://github.com/gabadi/drywall/releases/latest/download/drywall-macos-x86_64 -o drywall
chmod +x ./drywall
```

The Linux binary is statically linked (musl) — no runtime dependencies.

## Usage

```bash
drywall ./src
```

Exit codes: `0` = no duplicates, `1` = duplicates found, `2` = bad arguments.

## Use as a CI gate

Add to any project's CI pipeline:

```yaml
- name: Install drywall
  run: |
    curl -sL https://github.com/gabadi/drywall/releases/latest/download/drywall-linux-x86_64 -o drywall
    chmod +x ./drywall

- name: DRY check
  run: ./drywall ./src
```

The step fails (exit 1) if duplicates are found.

## Build from source

Requires Rust 1.96+:

```bash
cargo build --release
./target/release/drywall ./src
```

name: Release

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - 'v*'

env:
  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always

permissions:
  contents: write

jobs:
  build:
    name: Build ${{ matrix.target }}
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      matrix:
        include:
          - target: x86_64-unknown-linux-musl
            os: ubuntu-latest
            artifact: drywall-linux-x86_64
          - target: aarch64-apple-darwin
            os: macos-latest
            artifact: drywall-macos-aarch64
          - target: x86_64-apple-darwin
            os: macos-13
            artifact: drywall-macos-x86_64

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683 # v4.2.2

      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@3c5f7ea28cd621ae0bf5283f0e981fb97b8a7af9 # master
        with:
          toolchain: "1.96.0"
          targets: ${{ matrix.target }}

      - name: Install musl tools
        if: matrix.target == 'x86_64-unknown-linux-musl'
        run: sudo apt-get install -y musl-tools

      - uses: actions/cache@5a3ec84eff668545956fd18022155c47e93e2684 # v4.2.3
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cargo/registry
            ~/.cargo/git
            target
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-${{ matrix.target }}-cargo-${{ hashFiles('**/Cargo.lock') }}
          restore-keys: ${{ runner.os }}-${{ matrix.target }}-cargo-

      - name: Build
        run: cargo build --release --target ${{ matrix.target }}

      - name: Rename binary
        run: cp target/${{ matrix.target }}/release/drywall ${{ matrix.artifact }}

      - name: Upload artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4.6.2
        with:
          name: ${{ matrix.artifact }}
          path: ${{ matrix.artifact }}

  release:
    name: Create Release
    needs: build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Download artifacts
        uses: actions/download-artifact@d3f86a106a0bac45b974a628896c90dbdf5c8093 # v4.3.0
        with:
          merge-multiple: true

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@2bb465e97f322d3cb2a965294d483e0d26a67aa9 # v3.0.1
        with:
          files: |
            drywall-linux-x86_64
            drywall-macos-aarch64
            drywall-macos-x86_64
          generate_release_notes: true

# AGENTS.md

## Tool name mappings

Prompts use generic names; use the installed binaries:

- `ir-dry-checker` → `gherkin-ir-dry-checker`

`gherkin-ir-dry-checker` takes JSON IR from `gherkin-parser`, not the `.feature`:

```
gherkin-parser <feature> <ir.json>
gherkin-ir-dry-checker [--include-exact] <ir.json> <report>
```

`findings: []` = already normalized. Also installed: `gherkin-parser`, `gherkin-mutator`.

## Command index

| Purpose | Command |
|---------|---------|
| Install tools (once per machine) | `mise install` |
| Format | `cargo fmt` |
| Lint | `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` |
| Unit tests | `cargo nextest run --profile unit` |
| Property tests | `cargo nextest run --profile property` |
| Acceptance tests | `cargo nextest run --profile acceptance` |
| Language mutation | `cargo mutants --test-tool nextest -j 8 --no-shuffle -- --profile mutation` |
| Gherkin mutation | `gherkin-mutator --level soft --feature <feature>` |
| Coverage (≥90% lines) | `mise exec -- cargo llvm-cov nextest --profile unit --lcov --output-path lcov.info --fail-under-lines 90` |
| CRAP (threshold ≤6) | `cargo crap --lcov lcov.info --exclude 'acceptance/**' --exclude 'src/main.rs' --threshold 6 --fail-above` |
| Build release binary | `cargo build --release` |
| DRY self-check | `./target/release/drywall ./src` |

## Releasing

Tag-triggered only (`git tag v0.x.0 && git push origin v0.x.0`). See [README.md](README.md) for binaries, install, and CI usage.

## Mutation runs

**Always launch mutation commands with `run_in_background: true`.** Do not block waiting for mutation output — it takes several minutes. Check results when the background notification arrives.

## Tooling notes

- **gherkin-ir-dry-checker output**: use `rtk json <report>` to read the JSON report compactly rather than dumping full JSON into context.
- **gherkin-mutator**: `--feature-file` and `--feature-path` are wrong flag names and silently run 0 mutations; use `--feature`.
- **Build release binary before acceptance**: `cargo build --release` must run before `--profile acceptance` — stale binary causes false failures.
- **acceptance-entrypoint-generator**: requires `--steps <steps-file>` (basename only) for non-scaffold features; omitting it defaults to `scaffold_cli_steps.rs` and silently generates the wrong entrypoint.
- **cargo-mutants `--exclude`**: patterns match absolute paths, not CWD-relative. Use `**/filename.rs` form, not `src/filename.rs`.
- **acceptance/runtime/mod.rs `World`**: add `#[allow(dead_code)]` on the struct (not per-field) — runtime is `include!`-ed across multiple test binaries with differing step coverage.
- **Acceptance fixture paths**: must be outside gitignored paths (e.g. not under `tmp/`) — gitignore-awareness is default-on and silently skips ignored dirs, producing false-empty scans.
- **Ad-hoc QA scratch fixtures**: write to `/tmp` or a non-gitignored path; any fixture under project `tmp/` is silently skipped by drywall — produces false exit 0 mimicking a missing feature.
- **Parallel language test bodies (js_X / ts_X)**: differentiate source body structure, not just identifiers — identical short bodies trigger dogfood self-detection even when function names differ.
- **`Lang::Tsx` grammar**: must use `LANGUAGE_TSX`, not `LANGUAGE_TYPESCRIPT`; `typescript_grammar_rejects_jsx_markup` is the guard — removing or weakening it silently breaks JSX in .tsx.
- **acceptance-entrypoint-generator absolute paths**: when called with an output dir outside the project root (e.g. `/tmp/`), generated `include!` paths become absolute. Fix: write output directly into `tests/` or apply `sed` to relativize paths before committing.

## drywall output format

drywall output mirrors dry4go: per-function node counts, one function per line. Sort order for ties is unspecified upstream; drywall pins lexicographic tie-break to guarantee deterministic output for mutation testing.

lines: -Tag-triggered only (`git tag v0.x.0 && git push origin v0.x.0`). See [README.md](README.md) for binaries, install, and CI usage.
+Tag-triggered only (`git tag v0.1.0 && git push origin v0.1.0`). Tag must match `v*.*.*`. See [README.md](README.md) for binaries, install, and CI usage.
